msContents.summary:
[p][i]Articella[/i][/p] [p]Written in double column, the text and commentary together in a larger and smaller rounded gothic script, possibly English, with many contractions. Each section has a title heading on each recto in alternate blue and red lettering 'AMPH' 'PNOST', etc. Ornamental capitals, with marginal decorations in alternate blue and red, and red and blue: paragraph marks in alternate red and blue: some smaller capitals tinted with yellow.[/p]
[p][b]Contents[/b][/p]
[p]1. ff. 1r-4v Hippocrates, [i]Liber prognosticorum[/i][/p]
[p]f. 1r Omnis qui medicine studio artis seu gloriam ... f. 4v ... ordine preceptorum. Explecit liber prognosticorum.[/p]
[p]2. ff. 4v-10v Theophilus, [i]Liber urinarum[/i][/p]
[p]f. 4v Incipit Liber urinarum theophili. De urinarum differantia negocium ... f. 10v ... convenienter exposimus.[/p]
[p]3. ff. 10v-12r Philaretus, [i]De negocio pulsuum[/i][/p]
[p]f. 10v Incipit Philareti de negocio pulsuum. Intentionem habemus in presenti conscriptione ... f. 12r ... dicta sufficiant. Explicit Liber Philareti de negocio pulsuum.[/p]
[p]4. ff. 12r-26r Galen, [i]Tegni[/i][/p]
[p]f. 12r Incipit tegni Galieni archimetros de compositionibus signis et causis egritudinis consequentis. Tres sunt omnes doctrine ... f. 26r ... orationem in eis. Explicit liber tegni.[/p]
[p]5. ff. 26r-31r Hippocrates, [i]De regimine acutorum[/i][/p]
[p]f. 26r Incipit Liber de regimine acutorum. Qui de egrotantium accidentibus ... f. 31r conveniens est. Explicit liber de regimine acutorum.[/p]
[p]6. ff. 31r-32v Expositio terminorum[/p]
[p]f. 31r Medicina est sciencia regendi complexionem naturalem ... f. 32v ... accidintia lucidi.[/p]

objectDesc.supportDesc: 1 volumeff. i + 32 + i. 190 x 130mm. 36 long lines.

binding.p: ff. i + 32 + i. 190 x 130mm. 36 long lines.

bibl.title: Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library
bibl.author: S. A. J. Moorat
bibl.publisher: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine

custodialHist.custEvent: [p]Catalogue description modified in 2014. For original description, see S.A.J. Moorat, [i]Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library[/i] (London: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine, 1962-1973), vol. 1, pp. 58-59.[/p] [p]C. O'Boyle, [i]Thirteenth- and Fourteenth-century Copies of the Ars Medicine: A Checklist and Contents Descriptions of the Manuscripts[/i] (Cambridge : Cambridge Wellcome Unit for the History of Medicine, 1998; Articella Studies: Texts and Interpretations in Medieval and Renaissance Medical Teaching, 1), p. 43.[/p]

revisionDesc.change: Transformed Calm xml export to TEI xml file using a transformation scenario
Manual edits following automated Calm-TEI transformation
